articles.title: Francis Could Become MMA’s Biggest Villain
articles.description: Francis Ngannou may have a bigger opportunity outside the cage than inside it. Chael breaks down why Francis should lean fully into the role of MMA’s “real” heavyweight champion — and why the smartest move might be becoming the villain. It starts with a simple idea: the greatest fighters in combat sports history didn’t wait for people to crown them. They crowned themselves. Using Muhammad Ali and Floyd Mayweather as examples, Chael explains how repetition, confidence, and marketing can shape legacy just as much as wins and losses. Francis already has the aura, the size, and the presence. The question is whether he’s willing to say the part out loud. Should Francis walk out claiming he’s the true heavyweight champion? Should he openly call out Jon Jones and Tom Aspinall? Should he embrace the criticism and become the most obnoxious man in heavyweight MMA? Chael thinks there may be real power in that strategy. Because sometimes the most effective thing a fighter can do… is make people angry. Watch more breakdowns and fight analysis from Chael Sonnen. #UFC #MMA #FrancisNgannou #JonJones #TomAspinall #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Greatest boxer experiment 1:28 Why Muhammad Ali became “the greatest” 3:15 Floyd Mayweather copied the formula 4:18 Chael used it in MMA too 5:24 Francis Ngannou enters the conversation 6:41 The “lineal champion” opportunity 8:12 Why the claim would annoy people 9:46 The Mike Van Arsdale story 11:15 Francis already has the aura 12:26 Call out Jones and Aspinall 13:34 Why Francis should turn heel Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: What If Ronda Rousey Loses?
articles.description: Ronda Rousey is back — but the real question isn’t whether she can beat Gina Carano. It’s what happens if she doesn’t. Chael breaks down the strange dynamic surrounding one of the most talked-about comeback fights in years. Gina Carano has been away from competition for nearly two decades, yet her recent workouts created plenty of discussion. Ronda looked like old Ronda. Gina looked like old Gina. But the deeper issue isn’t technique, conditioning, or even style matchups. It’s the stakes. Ronda reportedly called for this fight. She picked the opponent, the situation, and the return. Fighters don't do that unless they believe they can win. But if the comeback story goes the other direction, the fallout becomes much bigger than a single result. Chael dives into the size difference, Ronda’s past comments on weight classes, Gina’s physical advantages, and the uncomfortable reality that sometimes the danger isn’t losing a fight — it’s what losing says afterward. Because comeback stories are easy when they work. The hard part is figuring out what comes next if they don’t. Watch more breakdowns and fight analysis from Chael Sonnen. #UFC #MMA #RondaRousey #GinaCarano #ChaelSonnen 0:00 The real question isn't the fight0:23 Gina's open workout reaction1:50 Does Gina look dangerous?3:15 Ronda's workout breakdown5:04 Chael questions judo in MMA7:36 Gina vs Ronda comparison9:08 The size issue matters11:12 Why Ronda took this fight13:12 What if Ronda loses?15:05 What happens next for Gina?Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Is Chimaev Staying at 185? #ufc
articles.description: Khamzat Chimaev has done everything right—but now comes the biggest decision of his career. In this clip, the debate isn’t just about whether Chimaev deserves another fight with Sean Strickland. The real question is bigger: is Chimaev even staying at middleweight? From a competitive standpoint, the rematch makes sense. The first fight was razor close, emotional, and controversial enough that fans immediately started asking for another one. But MMA is different from boxing. Boxing thrives on trilogies. MMA almost never goes all the way to a true three-fight series. That creates a strange situation. If Chimaev and Strickland fight again and the result flips, logic says there should be a rubber match. But historically, the UFC rarely operates that way. That’s why the business side matters just as much as the competitive side. And before any of that gets figured out, there’s another issue hanging over everything: 185 or 205? Because if Chimaev decides to move divisions, the entire middleweight picture changes instantly. This is one of the most important crossroads in MMA right now. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #KhamzatChimaev #SeanStrickland #UFC #MMA #Middleweight #FightTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: Francis Ngannou's the Best Heavyweight Alive?
articles.description: Dewey Cooper says Francis Ngannou is the greatest heavyweight fighter in the world today… but Chael Sonnen says the verdict still isn’t in. Chael breaks down why Francis remains one of the most fascinating figures in combat sports history. From the close win over Ciryl Gane to the shocking performance against Tyson Fury, Francis has done things no heavyweight MMA fighter was ever supposed to do. But there’s still one giant question hanging over his legacy: Jon Jones. Chael explains why the timing of Jones vs. Gane complicated Francis’ standing, how MMA math suddenly started working against him, and why Alex Pereira’s upcoming heavyweight move could unexpectedly change the entire conversation again. Then comes the bigger philosophical question: Can you still call someone the best in the world after they willingly walked away from the title? Chael looks at the business side of Francis leaving the UFC, why it may have been the smartest financial move possible, and why “greatest fighter” and “biggest winner” are not always the same thing. Because Francis Ngannou’s story may not be finished yet. #UFC #MMA #FrancisNgannou #JonJones #ChaelSonnen #AlexPereira #TysonFury 0:00 Dewey Cooper’s Francis Claim 1:45 Why Gane vs Francis Was Misunderstood 4:00 The Jon Jones Problem 6:15 Francis vs Tyson Fury Changed Everything 8:40 Did Francis Walk Away From Greatness? 11:00 Why the Verdict Still Isn’t Finalized Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Pereira’s Size Changes Everything #ufc
articles.description: Alex Pereira doesn’t just look like a heavyweight anymore—he looks like he belongs there. In this clip, the conversation centers around Pereira’s physical transformation and how naturally the added weight appears on his frame. Some fighters bulk up and it changes everything negatively. The size lands in the wrong places, movement slows down, and the body looks forced. Pereira had the opposite result. The added muscle looks evenly distributed from head to toe, making him appear stronger without sacrificing his athleticism. And the comparison to Ciryl Gane is what really stands out. When coaches started discussing current weights, the numbers revealed something surprising: Pereira and Gane are essentially the same size. Same height. Nearly the same walk-around weight. Only a few pounds apart once fight week adjustments happen. At heavyweight, that gap becomes almost meaningless. That’s why this conversation matters. Pereira moving up no longer feels like a middleweight-sized fighter chasing a dream. Physically, he looks built for the division now—and that changes the way fans and opponents view him. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #AlexPereira #CirylGane #UFC #MMA #Heavyweight #FightTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: The Chuck vs Tito Warning Sign
articles.description: Chael Sonnen finally gives his official prediction for Ronda Rousey vs. Gina Carano… and it starts with a warning. According to Chael, the biggest bet in the entire fight may have nothing to do with who wins. Chael breaks down why he actually appreciates the honesty surrounding this matchup, especially from Gina Carano, who openly admitted she isn’t returning because of some bitter rivalry or manufactured hatred. Meanwhile, Ronda has completely embraced the promotional side of the fight — creating headlines, stirring controversy, and turning the event into a spectacle. But once the cage door closes, Chael believes reality could hit hard. From weight-cut concerns to conditioning questions, Chael explains why five rounds is a massive mistake for this fight and why shorter fights have always worked better in crossover combat sports. Using Jake Paul’s past events as examples, he explains why smart promoters protect aging stars by limiting rounds instead of stretching fights beyond their limits. Then Chael gets into the actual matchup. Does Gina’s size become a real problem for Ronda? Does Ronda’s recent athletic activity in WWE and Hollywood stunt work matter? And most importantly… does this fight even make it to the championship rounds? Chael gives his betting order, his biggest prediction, and ultimately picks a winner. #UFC #MMA #RondaRousey #GinaCarano #ChaelSonnen #JakePaul #WomenMMA 0:00 Chael’s Official Prediction 1:40 Why Gina’s Honesty Matters 4:00 Ronda Understands Promotion 6:15 Why Five Rounds Is a Mistake 8:50 Chael’s Biggest Betting Prediction 11:20 Who Actually Wins the Fight? articles.title: How Do You Promote Strickland vs Chimaev 2?
articles.description: How Do You Promote Strickland vs Chimaev 2? Sean Strickland and Khamzat Chimaev may still be heading toward a rematch… but according to Chael Sonnen, there’s one massive problem: How do you promote it now? After the two fighters publicly buried the hatchet, Chael explains why the UFC suddenly lost the most valuable thing in fight promotion — personal grudge. And in combat sports, nothing sells better. Chael pulls back the curtain on how fight promotion actually works, from press conferences and weigh-ins to the hidden business behind the UFC’s biggest events. He explains why these events aren’t just promotional tools — they’re monetized content products that have to perform. So what happens when two fighters no longer hate each other? Using Ronda Rousey vs. Gina Carano as the perfect modern example, Chael explains how smart promoters create entirely new storylines when the original angle disappears. From fake company rivalries to manufactured controversy, the fight business has always been about finding a narrative people care about. That doesn’t mean the rivalry has to be fake. It just means the angle has to evolve. #UFC #MMA #SeanStrickland #KhamzatChimaev #ChaelSonnen #RondaRousey #FightPromotion 0:00 The Strickland vs Chimaev Problem1:55 Why Press Conferences Matter4:20 The UFC Sells Everything Twice6:10 Ronda vs Gina Is the Perfect Example8:30 Why Personal Grudge Sells Best10:20 How Sean and Chimaev Can Still Sell the FightBad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Francis Ngannou on Philipe Lins, leaving UFC, Anthony Joshua fight, losing his son | Daniel Cormier
articles.description: Daniel Cormier IS BACK with the DC Show, this time the champ sits down with MMA superstar Francis Ngannou ahead of his fight against Philipe Lins on Saturday's Netflix MVP card. DC talks to the former UFC heavyweight champion about his CRAZY JOURNEY from Cameroon to France as a young man, and what it took to get to Paris 14 months later. Ngannou talks about his upbringing in Cameroon, and why he had to work in sand mines at just nine years old. DC and Francis go through his MMA journey and how transitioned from boxing into MMA and then eventually become a UFC champion. Francis also talks about the tragedy of losing his son Kobe, and how both DC and Francis have dealt with losing children. And don't miss Cormier and Ngannou talk about his Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua boxing fights, a future head-to-head with Jon Jones, and the reason why he decided to leave the UFC for the PFL. Timeline: 0:00 - Welcome the show, Francis! 2:00 - Growing up in Cameroon 7:00 - Working in sand mines as a child 10:00 - Deciding to leave 16:30 - Love for fighting 19:00 - 20-hour journey across Sahara Desert 25:00 - Ending up in France 29:00 - Transition from Boxing to MMA 32:00 - Becoming UFC champion 42:00 - Why Ngannou left the UFC 45:00 - Losing son Kobe 55:30 - Boxing career 57:00 - Crazy story about Anthony Joshua fight 59:00 - Philipe Lins 01:02:00 - When will he retire? 01:06:00 - Francis' legacy All lines provided by @hardrockbet Follow me on all social media platforms! articles.title: Chimaev Opens as Favorite Over Strickland
articles.description: The betting odds are already out for a hypothetical rematch between Khamzat Chimaev and Sean Strickland… and Chimaev opened as the favorite. Chael Sonnen breaks down why that matters far more than people realize. After one of the closest fights in recent memory, Chael explains why the UFC suddenly finds itself backed into a corner. Sean Strickland now has two possible opponents Khamzat Chimaev or Nassourdine Imavov, and according to Chael, neither option is ideal. Because both are rematches. Chael reacts to the public perception surrounding the first Strickland vs. Chimaev fight, why the live crowd believed Sean won, and why Dana White’s reaction after the event may tell the bigger story. Then the conversation shifts to strategy. If Sean really does have a choice, which matchup gives him the best chance to stay champion? Which fight pays more? Which fight is actually easier? Chael explains why that’s exactly how champions are supposed to think — and why Sean needs to decide quickly before the UFC decides for him. There’s also a bigger issue quietly developing behind the scenes: the UFC’s betting partner is already leaning one direction. And once the odds hit the board… the story may already be written. #UFC #MMA #SeanStrickland #KhamzatChimaev #ChaelSonnen #Imavov #UFCNews 0:00 Chimaev Opens as the Favorite1:35 Did Sean Actually Win?3:45 Why Sean’s Options Are Both Bad6:10 The Problem With Rematches8:25 Why Sean Needs to Decide Fast10:40 The UFC May Already Be Leaning Toward Chimaev Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Strickland’s Strategy Nearly Broke Chimaev #ufc
articles.description: Sean Strickland may have delivered the smartest tactical performance of his career against Khamzat Chimaev. In this clip, the fight gets broken down round by round—and the turning point came early. The opening round looked exactly how people expected: Chimaev pushing wrestling pressure and dictating the pace. But once Strickland stuffed a key shot in the second round, the entire fight shifted. The adjustment was subtle but brilliant. By constantly backing up while staying active with the jab, Strickland limited Chimaev’s ability to explode into takedowns. Most fans saw retreating. What was really happening was tactical defense. Every step backward removed the wrestling entries Chimaev was waiting for. And Chimaev kept waiting. Throughout the championship rounds, he was hunting for one mistake—one reckless charge forward, one greedy exchange, one moment where Strickland planted his feet for the knockout. It never came. Strickland stayed disciplined for five rounds and forced one of the closest fights of Chimaev’s career. That’s what made this performance so shocking. An undefeated aura suddenly looked vulnerable. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, fight analysis, and real insight into the game. #SeanStrickland #KhamzatChimaev #UFC #MMA #FightBreakdown #FightTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: Chael Predicts Disaster for Ronda vs Gina
articles.description: Ronda Rousey vs. Gina Carano sounds massive on paper… but Chael Sonnen sees some serious warning signs. After speaking with Jake Paul about crossover fights, boxing, and the future of combat sports, Chael shifts his focus to one of the most talked-about fantasy matchups in MMA history: Ronda vs. Gina. And according to Chael, the fight may already have one major problem. Both women look incredible physically. Ronda looks lean, muscular, and explosive. Gina looks athletic, strong, and naturally bigger. But Chael explains why appearances can be deceiving — especially in comeback fights involving former stars long removed from competition. Using examples like Chuck Liddell vs. Tito Ortiz and Mike Tyson vs. Jake Paul, Chael breaks down why aging fighters can “look ready” while still being miles away from real fight shape. Then comes the bigger issue: the fight being scheduled for five rounds. Chael explains why that decision makes no sense, why shorter fights would actually increase excitement, and why boxing negotiates almost everything except the one thing that matters most — rounds. He also revisits the original stylistic matchup between prime Gina and prime Ronda, including the size difference, takedown dynamics, and the one weakness Ronda herself openly admitted years ago. And before it’s over… Chael makes one very bold prediction. #UFC #MMA #RondaRousey #GinaCarano #ChaelSonnen #JakePaul #WomenMMA 0:00 Chael Talks With Jake Paul 1:15 Ronda and Gina Look Incredible 3:05 The Chuck vs Tito Warning Sign 5:20 Why Five Rounds Is a Huge Mistake 7:45 Prime Gina vs Prime Ronda 10:15 Chael’s Biggest Prediction for the Fight Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The “46-Pound Weight Cut” Story Is Nonsense #ufc
articles.description: Khamzat Chimaev cutting 46 pounds in a week? That story never made sense. In this clip, the rumors surrounding Chimaev’s conditioning and exhaustion get challenged directly. Fighters understand weight cuts better than anyone, and the idea that a professional athlete would publicly claim something that absurd simply doesn’t add up. More importantly, weight cuts alone don’t explain what people saw in the fight. The real factor may have been emotional exhaustion. From the tension leading into the matchup, to the heavy security presence, to the intensity between both fighters, the atmosphere was different. High emotion affects performance. Adrenaline dumps, anger, pressure, and stress can drain a fighter far more than people realize—and unlike weight cutting, that impact can show up live inside the cage. What makes this even more interesting is how the fight ended. Respect replaced hostility. One fighter extended his hand after everything that happened, and in many ways, that may have revealed who carried the bigger psychological burden throughout the night. This wasn’t just physical fatigue. It was emotional warfare. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into the fight game. #KhamzatChimaev #UFC #MMA #FightTalk #MMANews #CombatSports #WeightCut

articles.title: The Truth About Zabit’s Retirement
articles.description: Zabit Magomedsharipov may be the greatest UFC “what if” of the modern era. Chael Sonnen looks back at one of the most unique and talented fighters the sport has ever seen, a fighter who had the skills, the creativity, the hype, and possibly even championship ability… but still walked away before ever fighting for a title. From Zabit’s electric UFC run to the rumors surrounding his retirement, Chael breaks down why fans were so captivated by him in the first place. The length. The style. The unpredictability. Few fighters ever felt as different as Zabit did inside the cage. But this conversation goes deeper than highlights. Chael reacts to Zabit’s recent comments about why he stepped away from MMA, including the belief that the UFC never intended to make him champion during the rise of dominant Russian fighters like Khabib Nurmagomedov and Islam Makhachev. And while Chael admits there may have been truth to some of those politics, he also explains why the truly great fighters never think that way. This becomes a bigger conversation about ambition, timing, opportunity, and what separates contenders from champions. Because being good enough to become champion… and wanting it badly enough… are not always the same thing. #UFC #MMA #Zabit #ZabitMagomedsharipov #ChaelSonnen #IslamMakhachev #Khabib 0:00 The Legend of Zabit1:35 Why Fans Loved Him3:30 Why He Never Got the Title Shot5:40 The UFC Politics Around Russian Fighters7:50 Chael’s Real Issue With Zabit10:05 Why the Greats Never Think That Way12:10 Did Zabit Really Want It? articles.title: The Problem With Chimaev vs Strickland 2
articles.description: Khamzat Chimaev says he wants a rematch with Sean Strickland… and now the UFC has a real problem. Chael Sonnen breaks down why this situation is far more complicated than fans realize. On one side, Chimaev has done everything right — he’s called out everyone, fought anyone, and never backed down from a challenge. On the other side, Nassourdine Imavov may have actually earned the next shot. And that’s where things get messy. Chael explains why rematches are always dangerous business in MMA, especially when the first fight didn’t exactly set the world on fire. From rankings politics to pay-per-view reality, this becomes less about who “deserves” it and more about what fight actually makes sense for the UFC. There’s also the bigger question nobody can answer yet: Is Chimaev even staying at middleweight? After weeks of talk about moving to light heavyweight, Chimaev suddenly calling out Strickland may have finally revealed the truth. Chael reacts to the callout, explains why post-fight emotions matter more than people think, and breaks down the impossible position the UFC now finds itself in. Because whether fans like it or not… somebody is getting a rematch. #UFC #MMA #KhamzatChimaev #SeanStrickland #ChaelSonnen #Imavov #UFCMiddleweight 0:00 Chimaev Wants the Rematch 1:20 Why This Fight Is Complicated 3:05 The Imavov Problem 5:15 Why MMA Hates Rematches 7:10 Chimaev Has Done Everything Right 9:00 The Real Question: 185 or 205? Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Chimaev’s Weight Cut Story Makes NO Sense
articles.description: Khamzat Chimaev’s weight cut story has taken over the MMA world… and Chael Sonnen says the math simply doesn’t work. After reports spread claiming Chimaev lost 46 pounds in a single week, Chael breaks down why that’s not just unlikely, it’s physically impossible. From real-world weight cutting experience to the confusion between pounds and kilograms, Chael walks through where the story may have started, why fans ran with it, and what the numbers actually show. But this conversation goes way beyond a bad headline. Chael also dives into what comes next for Chimaev after the confusion surrounding his return. Is Khamzat staying at middleweight to chase Sean Strickland? Is a move to light heavyweight still on the table? And if he does move divisions, should the UFC match him against Jiří Procházka? Then there’s the fight fans have wanted for years: Bo Nickal vs. Khamzat Chimaev. Chael explains why the timing finally makes sense, why the UFC avoided the matchup before, and why the rankings no longer stand in the way. This isn’t just about weight cutting. It’s about matchmaking, credibility, and where one of the UFC’s biggest stars goes next. #UFC #MMA #KhamzatChimaev #ChaelSonnen #BoNickal #JiriProchazka #SeanStrickland 0:00 Chimaev’s Math Doesn’t Work 1:25 Why 46 Pounds Is Impossible 3:28 The Pounds vs Kilograms Confusion 5:00 Chimaev’s Brother Explains the Weight 7:20 Why the “Short Notice” Excuse Fails 9:15 The Best Fight at 205 11:00 Bo Nickal vs Chimaev Finally Makes Sense Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Josh Hokit Picked a Fight With EVERYONE
articles.description: Boost Testosterone with OXFORCE! https://www.PrimeBrands.com! For a limited time the first 100 Men that purchase today get a FREE OXFORCE shirt! As men your testosterone boost is essential to get what you need in life! Visit and learn more at https://www.PrimeBrands.com Josh Hokit walked into the UFC press conference looking for problems… and according to Chael Sonnen, he found them. From Alex Pereira to Jiří Procházka to Derrick Lewis, Hokit spent the entire event provoking some of the most dangerous fighters in the world — and he did it completely alone. Chael breaks down why that matters. In a sport full of entourages, fake tough guys, and fighters hiding behind teams, Chael explains why Hokit’s willingness to confront elite fighters by himself completely changed the energy of the room. He also reveals the behind-the-scenes dynamics fans miss during these UFC media events and why certain fighters handle confrontation very differently than others. Chael compares Hokit’s approach to moments from PRIDE FC, explains Derrick Lewis’ strategy during the press conference, and details how Alex Pereira nearly found himself in a situation the UFC accidentally created. Then things got even crazier once Ilia Topuria unexpectedly inserted himself into the chaos. This wasn’t just trash talk. According to Chael, this was a masterclass in preparation, improvisation, and understanding how to command attention in the UFC. #UFC #MMA #JoshHokit #AlexPereira #JiriProchazka #DerrickLewis #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Josh Hokit Has Problems With Everybody 0:52 Brendan Fitzgerald’s Big Message 1:46 The Truth About UFC “Tough Guys” 3:02 Why PRIDE Had a Bullying Problem 4:15 Hokit Walked In Alone 5:02 Why That Changes Everything 6:04 Hokit vs Jiří Procházka 7:04 Sean O’Malley’s Different Approach 8:05 Derrick Lewis Was Prepared 9:13 Alex Pereira Saw It Coming 10:26 The UFC Made a Huge Mistake 11:34 Chael’s Story About Fedor 12:45 Pereira Tried to Ignore Him 13:50 Josh Took the “Alpha” Position 15:05 Why Pereira Couldn’t Stand Up 16:04 Ilia Topuria Changed Everything 17:02 Josh Hokit Passed the Test Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: INTERVIEW: Grant Dawson Responds To “Glass Jawson” Critics
articles.description: Grant Dawson joins Michael Bisping and Paul Felder after his latest UFC win to break down the chaos of his fight with Remi Barki, getting rocked, battling through adversity, and finding the third-round choke. Dawson opens up on being called “Glass Jawson,” proving his toughness, the politics of training at ATT with future opponents, and why he thinks Ilia Topuria may be the best fighter at lightweight. FULL EPISODE @bispingpodcast 🔒 Become a Member for special Perks, Exclusive Content and ultra-exclusive Live Chats: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCDrG2_1TcVkXKXXsD6Kjwig/join #bisping #ufc #mma #mixedmartialarts #michaelbisping #ufcnews #mmanews #ufc328 #grantdawson

articles.title: King Green Calls Out Moicano For A $50K Bitcoin Bet, Wants BMF!, Nate Diaz | INTERVIEW
articles.description: Michael Bisping and Paul Felder are joined by King Green after his latest win to talk about proving the doubters wrong, chasing the BMF belt, wanting to honour Nate Diaz, Jeremy Stephens missing weight, missing out on another bonus, and his wild idea for a $50,000 Bitcoin bet with Renato Moicano. They also break down Sean Strickland’s shocking win over Khamzat Chimaev, what went wrong for Khamzat, and what could be next at middleweight. articles.title: 'What's NEXT For Khamzat?' | UFC 328 AFTERMATH
articles.description: Michael Bisping reacts after Sean Strickland shocked the world, defeated Khamzat Chimaev, ended his undefeated record and became UFC champion once again. Bisping breaks down Strickland’s performance, Khamzat’s cardio issues, the shoulder injury Strickland revealed after the fight, why the rivalry cooled down after the final bell, and what could be next for both men — including possible fights with Nassourdine Imavov, Joe Pyfer, Paulo Costa, Jiri Prochazka and more. 🔒 Become a Member for special Perks, Exclusive Content and ultra-exclusive Live Chats: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCDrG2_1TcVkXKXXsD6Kjwig/join ▶️ Follow Michael on Social: https://www.instagram.com/mikebisping https://www.twitter.com/bisping https://www.facebook.com/mikethecountbisping ▶️ Edited by John Brannigan: https://www.instagram.com/johnbranniganuk https://www.twitter.com/johnbranniganuk #bisping #ufc #mma #mixedmartialarts #michaelbisping #ufcnews #mmanews #ufc328 #khamzat #khamzatchimaev #seanstrickland

articles.title: Sean Strickland Doesn’t Care about the UFC Belt 😳
articles.description: Sean Strickland says he doesn’t care about UFC belts or titles “bestowed by men.” 👀 Instead, Sean says gratitude is what truly matters in life. 🔥 A rare reflective moment from one of MMA’s most outspoken fighters. #SeanStrickland #UFC #MMA #Mindset #Motivation #Shorts

articles.title: Sean Strickland Reacts To Josh Hokit’s Controversy 😳🔥
articles.description: Sean Strickland reacts to Josh Hokit after the chaotic UFC Freedom 250 press conference performance that sparked major backlash online. 👀 Hokit drew criticism after confronting Alex Pereira and making headlines with his behavior during the press conference, leading to heated debate across the MMA community. 🔥 Now Sean Strickland weighs in with his thoughts on the situation, fight promotion, and whether Hokit went too far or simply created attention before fight night. 👇 Did Josh Hokit cross the line… or was this smart fight promotion? #SeanStrickland #JoshHokit #AlexPereira #UFCFreedom250 #UFC #MMA Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/

articles.title: Sean Strickland REACTS To Josh Hokit 😳🔥
articles.description: Sean Strickland gave his reaction to Josh Hokit after the chaotic UFC Freedom 250 press conference performance that had the MMA world talking. 👀 Hokit drew major criticism online after going at Alex Pereira and creating one of the most awkward and intense moments of the event. 😳🔥 Now Strickland is weighing in… and fans are split on whether Hokit crossed the line or simply sold the fight. 👇 Was Josh Hokit entertaining… or trying too hard? #UFCFreedom250 #SeanStrickland #JoshHokit #AlexPereira #UFC #MMA

articles.title: “Beautiful Fight!” DJ Reacts To Joshua Van vs Tatsuro Taira
Sean Strickland Says Khamzat TRICKED Him In Round 5 😳🔥
articles.description: Sean Strickland says Khamzat Chimaev caught him off guard in the 5th round at UFC 328… and Sean thought he might actually lose the fight because of it. 👀 According to Strickland, he trusted Khamzat in the moment — then immediately got furious with himself afterward. 😳 “I may lose this fight because I trusted Chimaev.” That final round drama makes this fight even crazier in hindsight. 🔥 👇 Did Khamzat almost steal the fight late? #UFC328 #SeanStrickland #KhamzatChimaev #UFC #MMA
